prompt_text: Weights & Biases vs Comet pricing and features
response:
# Weights & Biases vs Comet: Pricing & Features

## Pricing Comparison

| Feature | W&B | Comet |
|---------|-----|-------|
| **Free Tier** | Yes (generous) | Yes |
| **Free Projects** | Unlimited | Limited |
| **Free Runs** | Unlimited | Limited (100/month) |
| **Storage** | 100 GB | Limited |
| **Team Members** | Limited | Limited |
| **Cost for Teams** | $12-50/user/mo | Custom pricing |

## Core Features Comparison

### Weights & Biases
**Strengths:**
- ✅ Better UI/UX (generally considered more intuitive)
- ✅ Stronger community & documentation
- ✅ Native GitHub integration
- ✅ Better hyperparameter tuning (Sweeps)
- ✅ Model registry & versioning
- ✅ LLM-focused features (Weave)
- ✅ Larger ecosystem integrations

**Weaknesses:**
- ❌ Can feel feature-heavy for simple use cases
- ❌ Limited real-time collaboration features

### Comet
**Strengths:**
- ✅ More affordable for enterprises
- ✅ Better data governance & compliance
- ✅ Faster for some workflows
- ✅ Simpler UI (less overwhelming)
- ✅ Better for production monitoring
- ✅ Good for edge deployments

**Weaknesses:**
- ❌ Smaller community
- ❌ Fewer third-party integrations
- ❌ Less mature LLM features

## Quick Decision Guide

**Choose W&B if you:**
- Need comprehensive experiment tracking
- Work with LLMs/transformers
- Want the best hyperparameter optimization
- Prefer an active community
- Need model registry features

**Choose Comet if you:**
- Need enterprise security/compliance
- Want simpler, lightweight tracking
- Have production monitoring needs
- Prefer cost-effective team plans
- Need better data governance

## Bottom Line
**W&B** dominates in features and community. **Comet** wins on simplicity and cost for enterprises. Most teams pick W&B unless they have specific compliance/cost requirements.
